data.title: Analysis: Extreme market panic may indicate an approaching turning point.
data.description: According to Cointelegraph, current sentiment in the crypto market has fallen to "extremely depressed" levels, potentially nearing a "persistent bottom," with selling pressure likely to gradually weaken. The Bitcoin Fear & Greed Index shows that when the 21-day moving average falls below the zero line and then turns upward again, it often corresponds to a temporary bottom, and a similar signal has appeared. This typically indicates weakening selling momentum and stabilizing market conditions. However, the organization also cautions that prices may still fall further in the short term, but historical experience suggests that extreme negative sentiment often provides relatively attractive entry windows. Meanwhile, Alternative.me's Fear & Greed Index reads 10 (out of 100), placing it in the "extreme fear" zone, its lowest level since June 2022. Hive Chairman Frank Holmes stated that Bitcoin's current price is about two standard deviations below its 20-day moving average, a situation that has only occurred three times in the past five years. Historical data shows that in similar extremely oversold environments, a technical rebound is more likely to occur within the following 20 trading days. If Bitcoin closes lower in February, it will mark its fifth consecutive monthly decline, one of the longest losing streaks since 2018. The market is watching to see if extreme sentiment and oversold technical signals will combine to drive a new turning point.

data.title: Lending platform ZeroLend announces it will cease operations.
data.description: According to an official announcement, the decentralized lending protocol ZeroLend has decided to cease operations after three years. ZeroLend stated that several early-supported blockchains have become inactive or experienced significant liquidity declines, oracle providers have ceased support, and increased attacks from hackers and scammers have resulted in the protocol operating at a loss for an extended period. Currently, most markets have set their loan-to-value (LTV) ratio to 0%, and ZeroLend strongly advises users to withdraw any remaining funds on the platform as soon as possible. For on-chain assets with severely deteriorated liquidity, the team is developing a solution to upgrade the execution time lock to redistribute affected assets.

data.title: Polish Fintech Blik Considers IPO in Warsaw
data.description: Polish fintech company Blik is exploring the possibility of launching an initial public offering (IPO) in Warsaw. Bloomberg posted on X, citing a source familiar with the situation, that preliminary discussions have been held regarding the potential benefits of such a move. The company is evaluating the advantages of going public in the Polish capital, although no final decision has been made. Blik, known for its mobile payment solutions, is considering this step as part of its growth strategy. Further details about the IPO plans have not been disclosed.

data.title: Moonshot Aims for $10 Billion Valuation in Funding Round
data.description: Moonshot is seeking to achieve a valuation of $10 billion as it expands its current funding round. Bloomberg posted on X that the round has already received backing from major investors, including Alibaba and Tencent. The company is looking to leverage this financial support to further its growth and development. This move highlights the increasing interest and investment in the tech sector by prominent firms. Moonshot's ambitious valuation target underscores its potential and the confidence investors have in its future prospects.

data.title: Dogecoin Sees Weekly Bearish Cross: Bottom Or Breakdown Next?
data.description:
Dogecoin is flashing a rare weekly “bearish cross” just as traders debate whether last week’s $0. 08 washout was the cycle’s reset or merely the first leg lower. The setup matters beyond DOGE itself because memecoin flows are increasingly being treated as a proxy for risk appetite across crypto. Is The Dogecoin Bottom In? A chart shared by Charting Guy shows the 20-week EMA crossing below the 200-week EMA, a technical event he argues has historically aligned with DOGE capitulation
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/dogecoin/dogecoin-weekly-bearish-cross/

data.title: Zimbabwe's Inflation Rate Drops Below 10% for First Time Since 1997
data.description: Zimbabwe's annual inflation rate has decreased to below 10%, marking a significant milestone for the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this achievement as the first time since 1997 that Zimbabwe has experienced such a low inflation rate. This development is noteworthy for a nation that has historically been associated with hyperinflation and the issuance of trillion-dollar banknotes. The reduction in inflation is seen as a positive step towards economic stability in Zimbabwe.

data.title: STOCKS | Egypt's Stock Index Outperforms Emerging Markets
data.description: Egypt's benchmark stock index is experiencing significant growth, surpassing other developing-nation markets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing investor interest driven by government initiatives aimed at revitalizing the economy. These measures have positioned Egypt's market as a standout performer among its peers, attracting attention from both local and international investors.

data.title: Axis Robotics Achieves Breakthrough in Crowdsourced Data Collection
data.description:
Axis Robotics, a robotics startup, has announced a significant achievement in its experiment codenamed 'The Little Prince's Rose.' According to ChainCatcher, the company successfully mobilized 12,000 global users to collect 10,000 high-quality trajectory data entries within five days through its proprietary browser-based platform. This experiment demonstrated that building robotic intelligence can be achieved outside traditional, costly laboratory environments through public participation, leading to a large-scale data explosion.

Leveraging the MetaSim architecture, Axis Robotics developed an end-to-end pipeline from task setup to real machine deployment. The company decoupled complex physical simulations from lightweight web interactions, ensuring the collected data is capable of deep processing. The platform employs cross-simulation replay technology, replaying high-frequency physical data from MuJoCo in Isaac Sim and conducting large-scale domain randomization to enhance data diversity.

Currently, the company has successfully implemented real machine replication on the Franka robotic arm using the data provided by ordinary internet users, validating the feasibility of this data collection model for industrial applications.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 68,000 USDT with a 0.68% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 17, 2026, 05:24 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 68,000 USDT and is now trading at 67,990 USDT, with a narrowed 0.68% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Hong Kong International Airport's Terminal 2 Set to Open in May
data.description: Hong Kong International Airport's expanded Terminal 2 is scheduled to begin operations on May 27. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the airport's efforts to enhance its capacity and improve passenger experience. The expansion is part of a broader initiative to accommodate increasing air traffic and support the region's economic growth. The new terminal will feature advanced facilities and streamlined services, aiming to provide a seamless travel experience for passengers. This development is expected to bolster Hong Kong's position as a major aviation hub in Asia.

data.title: Danaher Approaches $10 Billion Acquisition of Masimo
data.description: Danaher Corporation is reportedly close to finalizing a deal to acquire U.S. medical technology company Masimo for approximately $10 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the negotiations. The acquisition would expand Danaher's portfolio in the healthcare sector, aligning with its strategic focus on medical technology. The deal is expected to enhance Danaher's capabilities in patient monitoring and healthcare solutions, though official confirmation from either company is yet to be announced. The move reflects ongoing consolidation trends within the medical technology industry, as companies seek to strengthen their market positions through strategic acquisitions.

data.title: AI Sentiment Shift Driven by Anecdotes, Not Evidence
data.description: The recent change in sentiment towards artificial intelligence is largely attributed to anecdotal evidence rather than concrete data. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the shift in perception is not backed by substantial evidence. This trend has been observed as discussions around AI continue to evolve, with narratives often shaped by personal stories and experiences rather than empirical research. The reliance on anecdotes has sparked debates about the validity and impact of AI advancements, as stakeholders seek to understand the true implications of these technologies.

data.title: Bitcoin Long-Term Holder Accumulation Slows Amid Market Stress
data.description: Bitcoin long-term holders have reduced their accumulation following the February sell-off, according to NS3.AI. Stress levels in the market are comparable to those experienced during the Luna collapse. The Long-Term Holder SOPR has dropped below one, indicating that veteran investors are taking losses. Market experts suggest that if the $65,000 support level fails, the next support could be at $54,000. However, a significant buy wall near $60,000 may offer short-term stabilization.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen Seeks Re-election Amid Crisis Management Reputation
data.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is leveraging her reputation for crisis management to secure another term in office. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Frederiksen's efforts to stand up for Greenland against U.S. threats, which has bolstered her image as a strong leader. Frederiksen, known as the "queen of crises," has navigated various challenges during her tenure, including international disputes and domestic issues. Her leadership style and ability to handle crises have become central themes in her campaign for re-election. As Denmark approaches the election period, Frederiksen's track record in crisis management is expected to play a significant role in her bid to retain her position as Prime Minister.

data.title: Venezuela Anti-Corruption Probe Shuts Down Crypto Exchanges and Mining
data.description:
Venezuela’s government ordered shutdowns of crypto exchanges and mining farms amid an anti-corruption probe and restructuring of the national crypto regulator, halting major operations and raising uncertainty for the industry.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/venezuela-anti-corruption-probe-shuts-down-crypto-exchanges-and-mining/

data.title: Backpack authentication before starting TGE
data.description: Backpack has officially announced the launch of identity verification before TGE, which is the first step in the process for users to claim their tokens. Users can now verify their identity through the website or app.

data.title: Tether and Circle's Investment Strategies Analyzed
data.description: Stacy Muur posted on X, highlighting the investment strategies of Tether and Circle. The post suggests that understanding the endgame for these companies can be achieved by examining their investment patterns. Tether and Circle, both significant players in the cryptocurrency market, have been actively involved in various ventures, which could provide insights into their long-term objectives. The focus on their investments may reveal strategic directions and potential impacts on the broader financial landscape.

data.title: Al-Khayyat Brothers Contribute to Syria's Reconstruction
data.description: The Al-Khayyat brothers, known for their innovative approach to ensuring Qatar's dairy supply by airlifting cows, are now focusing their efforts on the reconstruction of Syria. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting their significant involvement in rebuilding their homeland. The brothers' initiatives are part of a broader effort to restore infrastructure and economic stability in the war-torn country. Their contributions are seen as a vital step in Syria's recovery process, aiming to revitalize key sectors and improve living conditions for its citizens.
